steps.title: Combine the terms
steps.content:
Start by moving the $a$-terms to the same side:
$$4a=\frac{9b^2}{a}-12a$$
$$16a=\frac{9b^2}{a}$$

steps.title: Take the positive root
steps.content:
Divide by $16$ and take the square root. Since $a$ and $b$ are positive, use the positive root:
$$a^2=\frac{9b^2}{16}$$
$$a=\frac{3b}{4}$$
The answer is C.
